sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content:
Primary treatment for early-stage disease (stages IA, IB1, IB2, select IIA1) with curative intent. Also used for fertility preservation in select patients. Salvage surgery (pelvic exenteration) is an option for central pelvic recurrence after prior RT. Surgical staging via lymphadenectomy informs adjuvant therapy.

Principles:
Treatment is stratified by stage and histology (CERV 1-5).
Fertility-sparing approaches (conization, trachelectomy) are options for highly selected patients with early-stage, low-risk disease (Stage IA1 with no LVSI, Stage IA2-IB1 meeting conservative criteria). Consultation with REI experts is suggested.
Querleu and Morrow classification (Type A, B, C) describes the extent of hysterectomy. Open abdominal approach is standard (Category 1). Minimally invasive radical hysterectomy is associated with lower DFS and OS in a randomized trial (LACC).
Sentinel lymph node (SLN) mapping is recommended for early-stage disease; best detection in tumors <2 cm. Adherence to the SLN algorithm (side-specific dissection for failed mapping) is critical.
Para-aortic lymphadenectomy is recommended for ≥Stage IB1 disease.
Pelvic exenteration for recurrence should be performed at high-volume centers.

Resectability Criteria:
Surgical candidacy is determined by medical operability, disease extent (stage, absence of distant metastases), and patient factors. For fertility-sparing surgery, strict criteria must be met: tumor size ≤2 cm, no LVSI (preferred), negative margins, specific histology (squamous any grade, usual type adenocarcinoma G1-2), negative imaging for locoregional disease.

Pre-operative Optimisation:
General pre-operative assessment and optimization of comorbidities. Consider fertility counseling and REI referral for candidates for fertility-sparing surgery. Smoking cessation is advised.

cards.name: Cone Biopsy (Conization)
cards.detail:
Approach: Cold knife conization (CKC) preferred; LEEP acceptable if margins/orientation adequate.
Indications: Diagnostic staging for microinvasive disease; fertility-sparing treatment for Stage IA1 (no LVSI); part of conservative surgery for Stage IA2-IB1 meeting criteria.
Key Complications: Hemorrhage, infection, cervical stenosis, preterm delivery in future pregnancies.
Operative Mortality: <1%

cards.name: Type A Hysterectomy (Simple/Extrafascial)
cards.detail:
Approach: Vaginal, laparotomy, or minimally invasive.
Indications: Non-fertility-sparing treatment for Stage IA1 (no LVSI, negative margins); treatment for Stage IA2-IB1 meeting all conservative surgery criteria (CERV-4).
Key Complications: Hemorrhage, infection, urinary/rectal injury, vaginal cuff dehiscence.
Operative Mortality: <1%

cards.name: Type B Radical Hysterectomy (Modified Radical)
cards.detail:
Approach: Laparotomy.
Indications: Stage IA1 with LVSI and positive carcinoma margins; some cases of Stage IA2-IB1.
Key Complications: Increased risk of urinary retention, ureteral injury, lymphedema compared to Type A.
Operative Mortality: <1%

cards.name: Type C1 Radical Hysterectomy
cards.detail:
Approach: Laparotomy (Category 1).
Indications: Stage IB1 (not meeting conservative criteria), IB2, IIA1 (Category 1). Nerve-sparing.
Key Complications: Urinary retention, bladder dysfunction, lymphedema, sexual dysfunction.
Operative Mortality: <1%

cards.name: Radical Trachelectomy (Vaginal or Abdominal)
cards.detail:
Approach: Vaginal or laparotomy.
Indications: Fertility-sparing for Stage IB1 (not meeting conservative criteria), select IB2 (tumors <2 cm).
Key Complications: Cervical stenosis, incompetence, preterm birth, need for adjuvant therapy.
Operative Mortality: <1%

cards.name: Pelvic Exenteration (Anterior, Posterior, Total)
cards.detail:
Approach: Laparotomy, often with intraoperative RT (IORT).
Indications: Central pelvic recurrence after prior RT, no distant metastasis.
Key Complications: High morbidity (ureteral complications, fistula, infection), psychosocial impact.
Operative Mortality: ≤5%
